infobox.value: (大和国, *Yamato no Kuni*; Japanese pronunciation: [[jaꜜ.ma.to (no kɯ.ɲi)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Japanese "Help:IPA/Japanese")[[1]](#cite_note-1)) was a [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Japan "Provinces of Japan") of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), located in [Kinai](/wiki/Kinai "Kinai"), corresponding to present-day [Nara Prefecture](/wiki/Nara_Prefecture "Nara Prefecture") in [Honshū](/wiki/Honsh%C5%AB "Honshū").[[2]](#cite_note-2) It was also called **Washū** (和州). Yamato consists of two characters, 大 "great", and 和 "[Wa](/wiki/Wa_(name_of_Japan) "Wa (name of Japan)")". At first, the name was written with one different character (大[倭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%80%AD "wikt:倭")), but due to its [offensive connotation](/wiki/Graphic_pejoratives_in_written_Chinese "Graphic pejoratives in written Chinese"), for about ten years after 737, this was revised to use more desirable characters (大[養](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A4%8A "wikt:養")[徳](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%B3 "wikt:徳")) (see [Names of Japan](/wiki/Names_of_Japan "Names of Japan")). The final revision was made in the second year of the [Tenpyō-hōji](/wiki/Tenpy%C5%8D-h%C5%8Dji "Tenpyō-hōji") era (c. 758). It is classified as a great province in the *[Engishiki](/wiki/Engishiki "Engishiki")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former province of Japan

| Yamato Province 大和国 | |
| --- | --- |
| [Province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Japan "Provinces of Japan") of Japan | |
| 7th century–1871 | |
| Map of Japanese provinces (1868) with Yamato Province highlighted | |
| Capital | [Takaichi District](/wiki/Takaichi_District,_Nara "Takaichi District, Nara") |
|  | |
| History |  |
|  | |
| • Established | 7th century |
| • Disestablished | 1871 |
|  | |
|  | |
|  | |
| Today part of | [Nara Prefecture](/wiki/Nara_Prefecture "Nara Prefecture") |

**Yamato Province** (大和国, *Yamato no Kuni*; Japanese pronunciation: [[jaꜜ.ma.to (no kɯ.ɲi)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Japanese "Help:IPA/Japanese")[[1]](#cite_note-1)) was a [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Japan "Provinces of Japan") of [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), located in [Kinai](/wiki/Kinai "Kinai"), corresponding to present-day [Nara Prefecture](/wiki/Nara_Prefecture "Nara Prefecture") in [Honshū](/wiki/Honsh%C5%AB "Honshū").[[2]](#cite_note-2) It was also called **Washū** (和州). Yamato consists of two characters, 大 "great", and 和 "[Wa](/wiki/Wa_(name_of_Japan) "Wa (name of Japan)")". At first, the name was written with one different character (大[倭](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%80%AD "wikt:倭")), but due to its [offensive connotation](/wiki/Graphic_pejoratives_in_written_Chinese "Graphic pejoratives in written Chinese"), for about ten years after 737, this was revised to use more desirable characters (大[養](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%A4%8A "wikt:養")[徳](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BE%B3 "wikt:徳")) (see [Names of Japan](/wiki/Names_of_Japan "Names of Japan")). The final revision was made in the second year of the [Tenpyō-hōji](/wiki/Tenpy%C5%8D-h%C5%8Dji "Tenpyō-hōji") era (c. 758). It is classified as a great province in the *[Engishiki](/wiki/Engishiki "Engishiki")*.

The [Yamato Period](/wiki/Yamato_Period "Yamato Period") in the [history of Japan](/wiki/History_of_Japan "History of Japan") refers to the late [Kofun Period](/wiki/Kofun_Period "Kofun Period") (c. 250–538) and [Asuka Period](/wiki/Asuka_Period "Asuka Period") (538–710). Japanese [archaeologists](/wiki/Archaeologists "Archaeologists") and historians emphasize the fact that during the early Kofun Period the [Yamato Kingship](/wiki/Yamato_Kingship "Yamato Kingship") was in close contention with other regional powers, such as [Kibi Province](/wiki/Kibi_Province "Kibi Province") near present-day [Okayama Prefecture](/wiki/Okayama_Prefecture "Okayama Prefecture"). Around the 6th century, the local chieftainship gained national control and established the Imperial court in Yamato Province.

The [battleship](/wiki/Battleship "Battleship") [*Yamato*](/wiki/Japanese_battleship_Yamato "Japanese battleship Yamato"), the flagship of the [Japanese Combined Fleet](/wiki/Japanese_Combined_Fleet "Japanese Combined Fleet") during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), was named after this province.

## Capital

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yamato_Province&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Capital")]

During the [Kofun period](/wiki/Kofun_period "Kofun period") (300 to 538) and the [Asuka period](/wiki/Asuka_period "Asuka period"), many palace capitals were located in [Kashihara](/wiki/Kashihara,_Nara "Kashihara, Nara"), [Asuka](/wiki/Asuka,_Nara "Asuka, Nara"), and [Sakurai](/wiki/Sakurai,_Nara "Sakurai, Nara"). Yamato was the first central government of the unified country in the [Kofun period](/wiki/Kofun_period "Kofun period").[[3]](#cite_note-kofun-3) [Heijō-kyō](/wiki/Heij%C5%8D-ky%C5%8D "Heijō-kyō") capital was placed in [Nara City](/wiki/Nara_(city) "Nara (city)") during the Nara period.

In the 14th century, the capital of the [Southern Court](/wiki/Southern_Court "Southern Court") was established in [Yoshino](/wiki/Yoshino,_Nara "Yoshino, Nara") and Anou.

## Temples

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yamato_Province&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Temples")]

The [provincial temple](/wiki/Provincial_temple "Provincial temple") for monks is popularly thought to have been [Tōdai-ji](/wiki/T%C5%8Ddai-ji "Tōdai-ji"), but it may have in fact been a different one in Kashihara. The one for nuns was [Hokke-ji](/wiki/Hokke-ji "Hokke-ji").

The primary shinto shrine was [Sakurai](/wiki/Sakurai,_Nara "Sakurai, Nara")'s [Ōmiwa Shrine](/wiki/%C5%8Cmiwa_Shrine "Ōmiwa Shrine"), but there have been no records stating as such found at the shrine itself. There were no [secondary shrines](/wiki/Ninomiya_(shrine) "Ninomiya (shrine)"). The [sōja](/wiki/S%C5%8Dja_shrine "Sōja shrine") (or principal Shinto shrine in the province) was [Kokufu Shrine](/w/index.php?title=Kokufu_Shrine&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kokufu Shrine (page does not exist)") (Takatori, [Takaichi](/wiki/Takaichi_District,_Nara "Takaichi District, Nara"), [Nara](/wiki/Nara_Prefecture "Nara Prefecture")).
